name: Blackened Redfish
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the seasoning mix:
- 3/4 tsp Sweet paprika
- 3/4 tsp Ground white pepper
- 2-1/2 tsp Salt
- 3/4 tsp Ground black pepper
- 1 tsp Onion powder
- 1/2 tsp Dried thyme leaves
- 1 tsp Garlic powder
- 1/2 tsp Ground cayenne pepper
2. Heat a large cast-iron skillet over very high heat until it is beyond the smoking stage and you see white ash in the skillet bottom. Cook for at least 10 minutes.
3. Pour 2 tablespoons melted butter into each of 6 small ramekins. Set aside and keep warm. Reserve the remaining butter in its skillet. Preheat serving plates in a 250°F oven.
4. Thoroughly combine the seasoning mix ingredients in a small bowl.
5. Dip each fillet in the reserved melted butter so that both sides are well coated. Sprinkle the seasoning mix generously and evenly on both sides of the fillets, patting by hand.
6. Place the fillets in the hot skillet and pour 1 teaspoon melted butter on top of each fillet (be careful, as the butter may flame up). Cook, uncovered, over the same high heat until the underside looks charred, about 2 minutes (the time will vary according to the fillet's thickness and the heat of the skillet).
7. Turn the fish over and pour 1 teaspoon butter on top. Cook until the fish is done, about 2 more minutes.
8. Repeat with remaining fillets.
9. To serve, place one fillet and a ramekin of butter on each heated serving plate.
